title: Enforce lease write ownership and attribute foreign writes
goal: Turn a Grabowski write lease from an advisory coordination record into an enforceable or at least complete attribution boundary so an unknown same-user process cannot silently rewrite a leased worktree during review.

acceptance.assertion: A leased write scope is protected by an enforceable writer token, brokered mutation surface, filesystem watcher gate or equivalent mechanism with a documented threat boundary.

Every observed write in the protected scope is attributable to lease owner, process identity and audited operation, or is emitted immediately as an unattributed conflict with path, hashes and timestamps.
Head-/diff-bound review cannot proceed while unattributed post-snapshot writes exist; a stable snapshot receipt proves no later write before commit or publication.
Documented parallel work remains possible only after file, component, resource, process and deploy-path non-overlap is proven; unclear overlap stays blocked.
Tests cover cooperating writers, an unaudited same-user writer, symlink/path escape, atomic rename, editor temp files, lease expiry, owner crash and nonconflicting parallel scopes.
Detection never kills or rewrites a foreign process automatically; it freezes publication, preserves evidence and provides a bounded recovery path.

metadata.observation: An unknown process repeatedly modified exact files inside the leased isolated heim-pc worktree without matching Grabowski task, process or write-audit evidence.
metadata.risk: A clean review can be invalidated after inspection while existing lease evidence misleadingly suggests exclusive ownership.
